instruction: Summarize the CVE description in clear, professional cybersecurity language.
input: Multiple use-after-free vulnerabilities exist in the VCD get_vartoken realloc functionality of GTKWave 3.3.115. A specially crafted .vcd file can lead to arbitrary code execution. A victim would need to open a malicious file to trigger these vulnerabilities.This vulnerability concerns the use-after-free when triggered via the GUI's recoder (default) VCD parsing code.
output: The GTKWave 3.3.115 application has multiple use-after-free vulnerabilities in its VCD file handling, specifically within the get_vartoken realloc function. These vulnerabilities allow for arbitrary code execution when a victim opens a maliciously crafted .vcd file using the application's default GUI parser.
